steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must complete the researchers’ conclusion. The study found that several moth genera emit disruptive ultrasound and are also unpalatable (chemically defended). The hypothesis to be evaluated: ultrasound evolved because it costs less metabolically than producing noxious chemicals — implying the two defenses are alternatives, not complements.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'further investigations... will likely find that moth genera relying on this mechanism are also generally inedible to bats': This turns the finding into a prediction about future research rather than drawing an implication for the existing hypothesis. The study already found this overlap — it’s not a prediction. ✗
  • Choice B — 'most genera... do so primarily for purposes other than evading capture': The study says nothing about primary purpose; it measured acoustic properties and palatability, not motivation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the hypothesis about the development of this ultrasonic defense likely does not account for all instances of the trait in moths': Moths that already maintain chemical defenses couldn’t have evolved ultrasound to avoid that metabolic cost — they bear both costs simultaneously. The finding shows the cost-saving rationale doesn’t apply in every case. ✓
  • Choice D — 'unpalatability and echolocation disruption are not mutually exclusive traits': This restates the finding (moths can have both) without naming what that means for the hypothesis. The blank must draw a logical implication, not just echo the data. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
